claims.claim: Finland's governing right-wing coalition proposed dismantling the Cold War-era legal prohibition on nuclear weapons, with amendments to both the Nuclear Energy Act and the Criminal Code
claims.reasoning: Traceable to source [3] (Defense News) and reported by all three agents

claims.claim: Defence Minister Antti Häkkänen announced the 1980s-era blanket ban was no longer compatible with Finland's obligations as a NATO member
claims.reasoning: Traceable to source [1] (POLITICO) and reported by all three agents

claims.claim: The proposed amendments would permit nuclear weapons in the context of Finland's homeland defence, NATO collective defence, or defence cooperation
claims.reasoning: Traceable to source [3] and reported by all three agents

claims.claim: Criminal prohibitions on manufacture and detonation of nuclear devices would be retained
claims.reasoning: Traceable to source [3] and reported by all three agents

claims.claim: President Alexander Stubb defended the move as necessary to remove 'law-based obstacles hampering the defence and deterrence by NATO'
claims.reasoning: Traceable to source [2] (Daily Finland) and reported by all three agents

claims.claim: The government does not seek to host nuclear weapons and NATO has indicated no such deployment is planned
claims.reasoning: Traceable to source [2] and reported by all three agents

claims.claim: The main opposition SDP, Green League, and Left Alliance all registered objections
claims.reasoning: Traceable to source [2] and reported by all three agents

claims.claim: SDP chair Antti Lindtman argued the change would distance Finland from the nuclear policies of its Nordic neighbours
claims.reasoning: Traceable to source [3] and reported by all three agents

claims.claim: Tytti Erästö of SIPRI noted that Finland had been the only Nordic country with a full statutory prohibition on nuclear weapons
claims.reasoning: Traceable to source [2] and reported by all three agents

claims.claim: Laura Lodenius of the Peace Union disputed the security rationale

claims.claim: Opposition leaders were informed only one day before the public announcement
